title: Async Status Canvas (primitive)

statusHistory.reason: Engagement canvas live at /engagement/:slug. Sample Tremblay landscaping engagement (composite, in-progress) demonstrates the 5-stage status bar, chronological message thread (with marc/client/system authors and update/decision/question/system types), and live preview iframe slot. Read-only public sample today; auth-gated client view planned for backend phase.

problem: Clients of conventional consultants get sporadic email updates and scheduled milestone meetings, with the actual work-in-progress hidden between checkpoints. The async-status-canvas makes the work continuously visible inside the portal: status flags show stage (triage, planning, building, ready-for-review, shipped), a chronological message thread shows all communication, and a live preview iframe lets the client poke at the actual deliverable from day 2 onward. Replaces "demo day" with "demo always." Closes the trust gap that async-only consulting otherwise opens. Without this primitive, async feels cold; with it, async feels more attentive than a hurried real-time meeting.
analysis: The engagement page replaces both the 'project status email' and the 'demo day meeting.' Status flags ('triage' / 'planning' / 'building' / 'review' / 'shipped') visible at the top of every engagement page — the client always knows where things are. Chronological message thread below — every communication between client and consultant lives in one place. Live preview iframe embedded in the page — for engagements with a deployable artifact, the work-in-progress is poke-able from day 2 onward. The client never has to wait for a milestone meeting to see progress. Marc never has to write 'just checking in' status emails — the page IS the status. Without this primitive, async-only consulting feels cold; with it, async feels more attentive than a hurried real-time meeting.

decisions.decision: Status flags are visible globally; the client can never be confused about engagement stage
decisions.rationale: Async without status visibility = ghosting feeling. A persistent stage indicator at the top of every engagement page resolves this without requiring proactive status emails.
decisions.recommendation: 5 stages: triage / planning / building / review / shipped. Each transition is a structured event with timestamp, no free-text required. Optional substages within building if useful.

decisions.decision: Live preview iframe embedded from day 2 onward where deployable
decisions.rationale: Insight #21 — démos testables tout le long is a public commitment. The iframe binds Marc to ship a working preview from day 2. Failure mode F2 (live demo breaks) is mitigated by boring-tech project selection.
decisions.recommendation: Iframe with sandbox attribute. If the deployable can't be safely iframed (e.g., admin tools, native mobile), substitute with screen-recording-style preview updated weekly.

decisions.decision: Threaded messages are the only client-consultant communication channel
decisions.rationale: Email and phone are explicitly excluded by the architecture. Forcing all communication through the canvas keeps async-only enforceable and creates a complete audit trail for the showcase later.
decisions.recommendation: Notification emails are read-only — replies must be via the portal. Email reply attempts trigger an auto-reply: 'please reply via the portal.' Same for phone if a number is ever exposed.

successCriteria:
- Client always sees current engagement stage via persistent status flag
- Live preview iframe is interactive from day 2 of building stage onward (where applicable)
- All messages between client and consultant live in a single chronological thread on the engagement page
- No external communication channels — email reply attempts redirect to portal
- Threaded message + status history is sufficient to reconstruct the entire engagement narrative for showcase publication later
- Reusable as a pattern in any project tracking long-running async collaborations
implementation: Engagement model in src/lib/engagements.ts: { slug, client, title, problem, tier, startedDate, primaryLang, livePreviewUrl, livePreviewNote, stages[], messages[], relatedShowcaseSlug? }. Stages are an ordered array of { stage, date, completed, current }. Messages are chronological { id, date, type, author, body }. Type ∈ update|decision|question|system; author ∈ marc|client|system. Renderer src/pages/Engagement.tsx assembles three components: EngagementStatusBar (5-stage flag bar with past/current/future visual states — anchored in Insight #10 portal-as-canvas), EngagementThread (chronological message list — replaces email AND meetings; messages are decision-pinnable via type='decision' and visually distinguished), EngagementPreview (iframe slot with placeholder copy when not yet deployed — anchored in Insight #21 démos-testables-tout-le-long as public commitment). One mock fixture today (Tremblay landscaping, composite, in-progress) so visitors see what their own engagement page would look like. Real client engagements would be auth-gated; this fixture is the public sample.
userGuide: Visit /engagement/tremblay-paysagement-suivi-clients to see a sample read-only engagement page. The status bar shows where things are (triage / planning / building / review / shipped). The chronological thread shows every message between Marc and the client — no email, no meetings. The preview slot embeds the work-in-progress; if no live URL exists yet, a placeholder describes when it will appear (typically day 5 for Tier 2). To add a new engagement: edit src/lib/engagements.ts and append to ALL_ENGAGEMENTS. The route /engagement/:slug auto-renders. When an engagement reaches 'shipped', set relatedShowcaseSlug to the corresponding showcase to surface a 'view public page' CTA.
knownLimitations: No backend yet — engagement data is hand-written fixtures in src/lib/engagements.ts. Real client engagements would need auth + per-client data isolation.
Read-only on the public site. Real engagement pages would need an auth-gated reply form for the client to post messages.
No email-redirect-to-portal enforcement (the Insight #4 promise) — that requires a real email backend and inbound mail handling.
iframe sandbox is allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-forms — fine for self-hosted demos, too lax for untrusted iframe content.
Engagement-to-showcase handoff is just a slug pointer; the brainstorm Insight #40 redaction-on-request flow isn't wired (would need a per-client edit panel).

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial fill from brainstorming — primitive captures the portal-as-canvas principle (Insights #4, #10, #21).

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Instantiated as src/pages/Engagement.tsx + 3 components (EngagementStatusBar, EngagementThread, EngagementPreview) + src/lib/engagements.ts fixture. Sample Tremblay landscaping engagement (composite, in-progress) demonstrates the pattern publicly.
